title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
summary: Flash is a GPU cloud deployment tool that simplifies iteration by replacing Docker builds and server management with a single @flash.endpoint decorator on async Python functions, enabling hot reload and per-second billing. RunPod offers complementary pods for experimentation and serverless autoscaling for production workloads.
writeup: A live conference demo of RunPod's Flash Python SDK, which eliminates the commit-build-push-allocate loop that slows GPU model iteration by deploying a decorated async function directly from a local IDE onto RunPod's serverless GPU cloud. The presenter swaps Stable Diffusion models mid-session and then chains a three-model pipeline (Qwen 3 for prompt generation, DreamShaper for image synthesis, Nano Banana 2 for composition) to show that Flash's value scales with orchestration complexity. RunPod, founded in 2022 from spare crypto-mining GPUs, reached $120M ARR across 30-plus data centers in 10 countries.
key_points: Flash's core mechanism is a single Python decorator, @flash_endpoint, that packages only the decorated async function onto RunPod's GPU cloud while the surrounding orchestration code continues running locally, so the developer never leaves the IDE.

Hot module reload means any code change is immediately repackaged and re-deployed to the GPU worker without rebuilding a Docker image or re-provisioning infrastructure, collapsing the inner iteration loop to seconds.

The endpoint decorator accepts explicit GPU family targeting (e.g., Ada 80 Pro, which are Nvidia H100 variants), a max_workers ceiling (demonstrated at 5), and an active_workers floor (demonstrated at 1 to keep one worker always warm).
RunPod's serverless pricing charges only for the duration of an active request, not for idle time, so auto-scaling to zero between bursts means developers pay nothing when no inference is running.

A three-stage pipeline demo chains Qwen 3 (prompt generation on a public endpoint), DreamShaper (a Stable Diffusion 1.5 fine-tune optimized for illustrative art styles, 25 inference steps), and Nano Banana 2 (a Google photo-composition model) to illustrate that Flash handles multi-model orchestration, not just single-model calls.

RunPod was founded in 2022 from spare GPUs left over from a failed crypto mining venture, grew to $120M in annual recurring revenue by 2025, and operates 30-plus data centers across 10 countries with roughly 500 developers on the platform.
